question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that res.write and res.end are monkey-patched by the middleware to intercept all response data, and send requests that trigger very large response bodies, causing responseSize to accumulate to gigabyte-scale values that overflow JavaScript's safe integer range and corrupt responseSizeHistogram, masking a block delay attack?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: initMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that initMetrics() is called once at startup and all metric instruments are module-level singletons (allRequestCounter, durationHistogram, etc.), so that by flooding requests the attacker permanently corrupts the in-memory metric state, causing all subsequent exporter.collect() calls to be slow and delaying block processing by 500%+ of average block time for the lifetime of the process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a path that matches the metricsPath pattern (e.g., by guessing or brute-forcing config.metrics.config.uriPath) and, if authentication is disabled, repeatedly call exporter.collect() in a tight loop to block the event loop and delay block record ingestion by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the path normalization logic (`normalizedPath = pathname.endsWith('/') ? pathname : pathname + '/'`) by sending requests with trailing slashes to non-metrics endpoints, causing toOpenApiPath() to generate label values with trailing slashes that differ from non-trailing-slash versions, doubling label cardinality and accelerating memory exhaustion that delays block processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with Unicode or multibyte characters in the URL path so that Buffer.byteLength(chunk) in getChunkSize() returns a larger value than expected, causing responseSizeHistogram to record inflated values that skew monitoring dashboards and mask a block delay attack already in progress?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the 'finish' event handler captures `startTime` via closure and `Date.now()` is used for duration measurement, by sending requests that are artificially delayed server-side (e.g., by triggering slow database queries), causing durationHistogram to record values far exceeding the 500ms bucket boundary and signaling a block delay condition to monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: authenticate()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to the metrics endpoint with an Authorization header containing an extremely long username or password string, causing basicAuth(req) to parse a large header and tsscmp() to perform a timing-safe comparison on a very long string, creating enough CPU overhead per request to delay block processing by 500%+ of average block time when sent at high volume?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that inFlightCounter is an UpDownCounter (not bounded) by sending a large number of requests that are slow to complete, driving inFlightCounter to a very high value that causes monitoring systems to trigger false alerts about block processing delays, masking a real block delay attack?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with path segments that contain null bytes or control characters, causing toOpenApiPath() to store these as label values in metric instruments, potentially corrupting the Prometheus text format output from serializer.serialize() and causing the metrics scraper to fail, masking a block delay attack?

'0', 10) returns NaN or a negative number, causing requestSizeHistogram.record() to record an invalid value that corrupts the histogram state and delays block processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the monkey-patching of res.write and res.end to cause a reference cycle or closure leak, where each request's responseSize variable is never garbage-collected because the overridden functions hold a reference to it, causing heap memory to grow unboundedly and eventually triggering a GC pause that delays block processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: initMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the nodejs_process_cpu_usage_percentage gauge's addCallback captures previousCpuUsage and previousHrTime in a closure, and by triggering frequent metrics scrapes, cause these values to be updated so rapidly that the CPU percentage calculation becomes inaccurate, masking a CPU-exhaustion block delay attack?
